title: Rapid Bioburden and Sterility Testing for Biopharmaceutical Manufacturing
description: This project aims to develop and validate the Mango M6, a transformative system that addresses critical bottlenecks in biopharmaceutical manufacturing associated with bioburden and sterility testing.
text:
Traditional sterility methods following USP and 21 CFR standards require extended incubation of up to 14 days, significantly delaying the quality control process in biopharmaceutical manufacturing.
The Mango M6 offers a rapid, compact, and automated solution, leveraging lens-less microscopy and machine learning to deliver results in hours while maintaining compliance with regulatory standards.
By implementing real-time process monitoring and control systems in the Mango M6, an organization could reduce contamination-related losses by up to $57 million per fermentation run and $1–10 million per discarded chromatography lot, enabling significant material, product, and labor cost savings while ensuring faster and safer delivery of life-saving medicines to patients.
